reasoning:
The question asks whether Shakhriyar Mamedyarov will have an Elo rating on four different dates in 2025 that is >=1% higher than his baseline rating on April 13, 2025. Based on Wikipedia's FIDE rankings page, his April 1st rating is 2748. One percent of 2748 is 27.48, meaning his rating must jump to at least 2775.48 (so 2776). Gaining 28 rating points at the highest level of super-GM chess is an enormous hurdle. According to a Reddit post from early April, his live rating is only recently hovering around 2750, meaning he would still need a 26-point surge. At 39 years old, he has not had a streak that dominant in several years, and doing so by October 2025 would require near-miraculous tournament performances (e.g., winning multiple supertournaments with heavy plus-scores).

For the first resolution date (April 20), FIDE will not have published a new rating list yet, as they only do so on the 1st of each month. Thus, Wikipedia's table on April 20 will still reflect the April 1 list (2748), making it mathematically impossible for the condition to be met, capping my forecast at the floor value of 0.02.
For May 13, the May 1st list will be active, but it is deeply improbable he will gain ~26 rating points in just a single month of play. 
For July 12 and October 10, the timespan offers slightly more wiggle room, moving uncertainty up marginally, but the base rate of a 39-year-old ~2750 GM climbing to 2776 over 6 months without being an inactive dominant force makes this broadly close to 0%.
